response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack breadth, systems thinking, and notable low-latency experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as primary languages, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure a decade ago at National Instruments, with no recent Rust experience. Geospatial background via GISCollective is a real plus, but the core language mismatch makes this a stretch hire unless they are open to ramping someone strong in adjacent systems work.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (Sociomantic, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, distributed systems exposure
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; C/C++ work was at NI in 2014-2015
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the role's core languages
